description: Fix set_channel_topic and set_channel_purpose tools that crash with a TypeError because they access data.topic/data.purpose at the top level, but the Slack API returns the topic/purpose nested inside data.channel.topic/data.channel.purpose.

userStories.title: Fix set_channel_topic response parsing
userStories.description: As an AI agent using the Slack plugin, I want set_channel_topic to correctly parse the Slack API response so that the tool returns the topic metadata without crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: The slackApi generic type in set-channel-topic.ts expects { channel: { topic: { value: string; creator: string; last_set: number } } } instead of { topic: { ... } }
The handler reads data.channel.topic.value, data.channel.topic.creator, and data.channel.topic.last_set (with ?? fallbacks)
bun run build passes (run from plugins/slack/)
bun run type-check passes (run from plugins/slack/)

userStories.notes: File: plugins/slack/src/tools/set-channel-topic.ts. The bug is on lines 22-34. The Slack conversations.setTopic API returns { ok: true, channel: { ...full_conversation_object... } } where the topic is nested as channel.topic with shape { value, creator, last_set }. The current code types the response as { topic: { value?, creator?, last_set? } } and accesses data.topic.value — but data.topic is undefined because there is no top-level topic key. Fix: change the generic type parameter to { channel: { topic: { value?: string; creator?: string; last_set?: number } } } and change the handler to read from data.channel.topic instead of data.topic. Keep the ?? fallbacks for safety.

userStories.title: Fix set_channel_purpose response parsing
userStories.description: As an AI agent using the Slack plugin, I want set_channel_purpose to correctly parse the Slack API response so that the tool returns the purpose metadata without crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: The slackApi generic type in set-channel-purpose.ts expects { channel: { purpose: { value: string; creator: string; last_set: number } } } instead of { purpose: { ... } }
The handler reads data.channel.purpose.value, data.channel.purpose.creator, and data.channel.purpose.last_set (with ?? fallbacks)

userStories.notes: File: plugins/slack/src/tools/set-channel-purpose.ts. Identical bug pattern to US-001. The Slack conversations.setPurpose API returns { ok: true, channel: { ...full_conversation_object... } } where the purpose is nested as channel.purpose with shape { value, creator, last_set }. The current code types the response as { purpose: { value?, creator?, last_set? } } and accesses data.purpose.value — but data.purpose is undefined. Fix: change the generic type parameter to { channel: { purpose: { value?: string; creator?: string; last_set?: number } } } and change the handler to read from data.channel.purpose instead of data.purpose. Keep the ?? fallbacks for safety.
